title: The Fourth Precept
content:

{{Quote|The Fourth Precept by Stephan Martiniere: A fascinating examination of the nature of the Force.|A description of the comic in ''Star Wars Insider''|<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref>}}
"'''The Fourth Precept'''" is a five page [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] created by [[Stephan Martiniere]] and published in the [[2005]] anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''. The abstract comic has no dialogue and depicts a [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]] and [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark]] figure [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|dueling]] with [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] on a [[Universe/Legends|cosmic]] scale, representing the conflict for balance in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The concept for ''Visionaries'' was initiated by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler]], who wanted to highlight the works of the concept artists, including Martiniere, who worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] has  suggested that the [[canon]]icity of the comic within the Legends continuity was unclear.

==Publisher's summary==
''"''Powerful paintings of cosmic scale make up this abstract exploration of the fundamental forces of the galaxy in their unending battle for balance in the Force.''"<ref name="Preview" />''

==Plot summary==
[[File:LightDarkFourthPrecept.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The figures prepare to duel.]]
Two faceless [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] figures, one [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]], one [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark]], float inside a shiny sphere while holding [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and preparing to [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]]. They raise their [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] and begin to [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|clash]] while observed by a variety of giant creatures. The battle is intercut with images of active [[Volcano/Legends|volcanoes]], an [[Water/Legends|underwater]] environment, two colossal [[Species/Legends|monsters]] fighting, and [[Starship/Legends|starships]] departing the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The figures continue to fight while observed by a giant vessel with a skull in its center. The final page of the comic depicts an image of the [[Universe/Legends|universe]] followed by the two figures, who have both turned [[Color/Legends|blue]] and indistinguishable from each other. One stands holding a lightsaber while the other is behind them with [[Limb/Legends|arms]] outstretched; one hand is open while the other is a closed fist. The final panel portrays the [[Midi-chlorian/Legends|midi-chlorians]],<ref name="Fourth Precept" /> the microscopic [[Sentience/Legends|life-forms]] that grant individuals access to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}}</ref>

==Development==
{{Quote|Nevertheless, it seemed a shame not to give a voice to those artists who had perhaps more to say — to those who had become initiates in an arcane if popular artform. An obvious solution was to create a medium for this group to tell their own ''Star Wars'' stories. The result is ''Star Wars: Visionaries'' — intimate views by some of the key creators of a galaxy far, far away…|J.W. Rinzler, on the origins of ''Star Wars: Visionaries''|<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" />}}
[[File:Fourth_Precept_concept.jpg|thumb|left|150px|An uncolored illustration from "The Fourth Precept"]]
The five page comic "The Fourth Precept" was created for the [[Star Wars comic books|graphic novel]] anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''. The comic story lacks dialogue and was written,<ref name="Fourth Precept" /> illustrated, colored, and lettered by [[Stephan Martiniere]],<ref name="Legends" /> a concept artist who worked on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']],<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" /> which premiered on [[May 19]], [[2005]].<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> Author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler]] pitched the concept for ''Visionaries'' as a way for the film's artists to share their works and ideas<ref name="Insider 82" /> that would have otherwise not been publicly released.<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" /> Martiniere illustrated "The Fourth Precept" in [[2004]],<ref name="Fourth Precept" /> and the comic was announced on [[August 3]] of that year on a post on [[StarWars.com]], which also revealed Martiniere's involvement.<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> The title and summary of "The Fourth Precept" were unveiled alongside the titles of the other ten stories in ''Visionaries'' in another StarWars.com post on [[March 15]], 2005.<ref name="Preview" />

==Release and reception==
[[File:Stephan_Martiniere.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Stephan Martiniere created and illustrated the comic.]]
''Visionaries'' was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005.<ref name="Preview" /> "The Fourth Precept" is the sixth story in the collection, preceded by "[[Entrenched]]" and followed by "[[Prototypes]]." The comic is also followed by an outlined draft version of one of its pages.<ref name="Star Wars: Visionaries" /> Barlow expressed high praise for the works in ''Visionaries'', feeling that the artists involved had offered a variety of quality stories and works which justified their employment by<ref name="Insider 82" /> ''[[Star Wars]]'' creator<ref name="Year by Year" /> [[George Lucas]].<ref name="Insider 82" /> The entirety of ''Visionaries'' was later republished by Dark Horse in [[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2|''Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space'' Volume 2]]<ref name="Omnibus" /> on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "The Fourth Precept" was later published by [[Marvel Comics]] in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Legacy Vol. 4]]''<ref name="Legends" /> on March 15, [[2022]],<ref name="Amazon">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302934507|text=STAR WARS LEGENDS EPIC COLLECTION: LEGACY VOL. 4 TPB|nolive=1}}</ref> which placed the comic last in the collection, following "[[Celestia Galactica Photografica]]," another comic from ''Visionaries''.<ref name="Legends" />

==Continuity==
{{Quote|…the "The Fourth Precept" is anybody's guess,|Leland Chee, on the canonicity of the comic story|<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=125719&start=0675#10095628|thread=Event Timeline and Character Age Discussion|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-29|time=11:02 AM}}</ref>}}
[[File:LvDVisionaries.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The place of "The Fourth Precept" within ''Star Wars'' Legends was unclear.]]
When asked about the [[canon]]icity of the stories in ''Star Wars: Visionaries'' on the [[StarWars.com Message Boards]], [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] stated on [[March 29]], 2005 that "The Fourth Precept" was "anybody's guess" in regards to continuity, leaving its status unclear.<ref name="SWBoards" /> The comic portrays an abstract illustration of the conflict between the light and dark sides of the Force,<ref name="Preview" /> a concept created by George Lucas<ref name="Phantom Menace Scrapbook">''[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace Scrapbook]]''</ref> and portrayed across the ''Star Wars'' films.<ref name="Year by Year" /><ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref><ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> At the time of the comic's publication, the fourth precept of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]], first described in the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|first edition rulebook]] of ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' in [[1987]], was "There is no death, there is the Force."<ref name="Roleplaying">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']].</ref> 

As a part of the Expanded Universe at the time of publication, "The Fourth Precept"<ref name="Fourth Precept" /> would later be branded as [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] media in [[2014]].<ref name="Legends announcement">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref> ''Legacy Vol. 4'' of the [[Epic Collection]] line, which includes "The Fourth Precept," states that the stories in it take place more than [[Standard century/Legends|one hundred]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of the original trilogy film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]—which takes place in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]—thereby placing "The Fourth Precept" in or after [[104 ABY]], within the [[Legacy era]].<ref name="104 ABY" />

==Legacy==
The [[2019]] canon reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] mentioned a [[Poet|poem]] titled the [[Fourth Precept]], in which [[Deity|gods]] fought in a primeval [[universe]].<ref name="TROS dictionary">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>
